cards.title: HF Jobs: One-Command vLLM Server on HF Infrastructure
cards.why_it_matters: If you run evals or batch inference regularly, this gives you a reproducible, token-gated OpenAI-compatible endpoint on demand without managing your own GPU infra.
cards.summary: A single `hf jobs run` command launches a vLLM OpenAI-compatible server on HF-managed GPUs (e.g., a10g-large at $1.50/hr). The exposed port is proxied through HF's jobs proxy, gated by HF token. Scales to multi-GPU with `--tensor-parallel-size`; cancel explicitly to avoid billing.

cards.title: OLMo Hybrid vs. OLMo 3: Token-Level Analysis Reveals Where Recurrence Beats Attention
cards.why_it_matters: This controlled architectural comparison (matched data, tokenizer, training recipe) gives concrete, token-level signal on exactly where hybrid recurrent-attention models outperform pure transformers — directly relevant to architecture decisions.
cards.summary: Comparing 7B OLMo 3 (pure transformer) and OLMo Hybrid (attention + recurrent layers) at token level, HF researchers find the hybrid outperforms on semantically meaningful tokens (nouns, verbs, adjectives) and coreference-dependent tokens. The transformer's advantage emerges on verbatim-copy tokens where exact recall from context is sufficient.

cards.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation for Composing Image Generation Capabilities
cards.why_it_matters: Composing conflicting capabilities (T2I, local edit, global edit) in a single flow-matching model is a core training challenge; this paper offers a practical distillation framework for it.
cards.summary: DanceOPD routes each training sample to one capability field, queries a low-noise student-induced state, and trains with a velocity MSE objective within a flow-matching framework. Each capability is a velocity field over shared flow state space; the student learns on its own rollout states. The framework also absorbs operator-defined fields such as classifier-free guidance.

cards.title: RiVER: RL Training Without Ground-Truth Solutions Generalizes Coding Ability
cards.why_it_matters: Extending RLVR beyond ground-truth-labeled tasks is a key frontier problem; RiVER's transfer from heuristic optimization tasks to exact-solution benchmarks is a meaningful empirical result.
cards.summary: RiVER applies group-relative RL to score-based optimization tasks (AtCoder Heuristic Contest) using deterministic execution feedback without ground-truth answers. It addresses scale dominance and frequency dominance via instance-wise reward calibration. Trained only on heuristic tasks, it improves Qwen3-8B by 8.9% and GLM-Z1-9B-0414 by 9.4% on ALE rating rank, and also gains 2.4% and 3.5% on LiveCodeBench and USACO respectively.

cards.title: Autoregressive Boltzmann Generators: LLM-Style Architectures for Molecular Equilibrium Sampling
cards.why_it_matters: Applying autoregressive LLM architectures to physics sampling problems is a meaningful cross-domain transfer; the 60%+ zero-shot energy error reduction on 8-residue systems is a notable benchmark result.
cards.summary: ArBG replaces normalizing flows in Boltzmann Generators with autoregressive models, bypassing topological constraints and enabling sequential inference-time interventions. A 132M parameter transferable model (Robin) reduces zero-shot energy error (E-W2) on 8-residue peptide systems by over 60% versus prior SOTA. Gains are especially large on the 10-residue Chignolin benchmark.

cards.title: Sequence Probability Predicts Correctness Across Prompts But Not Across Decoding Choices
cards.why_it_matters: This clarifies when decoding tricks that push toward higher-probability outputs actually help — directly relevant to anyone designing inference pipelines, self-consistency schemes, or verifier-free self-improvement.
cards.summary: Across decoding methods, hyperparameters, models, and benchmarks, higher sequence probability is predictive of correctness when comparing different prompt-answer pairs within a dataset. However, increasing sequence probability by changing decoding hyperparameters or methods does not reliably improve accuracy, and probability is a poor indicator of correctness across repeated responses to the same prompt.

cards.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers Feed PDE Residual Directly as Input for Iterative Self-Correction
cards.why_it_matters: The finding that residual minimization is an unreliable proxy for reconstruction accuracy in ill-conditioned PDEs, plus a 10× gain on turbulent Kolmogorov flow, matters for anyone working on neural PDE surrogates or physics-informed ML.
cards.summary: ENS passes the PDE residual field as a direct input to the network at each iteration, letting it learn an update policy rather than optimizing residual as a loss. Across four PDE families it achieves highest accuracy in most settings, with up to 10× improvement on turbulent Kolmogorov flow. The correction policy generalizes zero-shot under distribution shift and cross-equation transfer, with the largest advantage in ill-conditioned regimes.

cards.title: Surface-Code Catalyst States Enable Exact Fine Dyadic Phase Gates with Constant Online Depth
cards.why_it_matters: Removing approximation error from non-Clifford gates while keeping online non-Clifford depth constant is a key milestone for fault-tolerant quantum computing; this protocol achieves both with a single verification round.
cards.summary: A new surface-code cultivation protocol creates reusable logical catalyst states implementing exact Z^{2^-b} phase gates via phase kickback, eliminating Clifford+T synthesis approximation error. A sqrt(T) = Z^{1/8} demonstration uses a nine-qubit brickwork Clifford circuit. At physical error rate p=10^-3, the postselected catalyst reaches distance-7 surface-code blocks with logical leakage ~10^-6 in ~7 attempts.
